Bastian Schweinsteiger in talks with Chicago Fire over new contract

Former Germany international Bastian Schweinsteiger says he is in talks with Chicago Fire over a new contract.

Schweinsteiger, 33, joined the Fire on a one-year-deal in 2017 and made 25 appearances in his debut season but is now a free agent. However, as he wants to continue his career, he has confirmed talks with the MLS club and is confident he will return for the new season.

"I want to continue playing football," he said at a PR event in Germany. "We are currently in talks. And I definitely want to continue playing football.

"I am always happy when I am out there on the pitch or working with my team in training."

Reina: 'No Juve, Milan or Napoli calls'
By Football Italia staff

Pepe Reina’s agent assures there have been no calls from Juventus or Milan, but they “haven’t heard from Napoli over a new contract either.”

The goalkeeper will be a free agent when his current deal with Napoli expires at the end of this season and turns 36 in August.

There have been reports in various newspapers that Juve and Milan wanted the Spaniard to be their understudy to younger shot-stoppers next term.

“Juventus and Milan have not called me and as of today I have not received any offers,” agent Manuel Garcia Quilon told Calciomercato.it.

“I haven’t heard from Napoli over a potential new contract either, but Pepe is calm and relaxed. He worked hard to have a great season with this club and to earn a place in Spain’s World Cup squad.”

Former Inter Milan and Sunderland midfielder Yann M'Vila has signed a one-and-a-half-year contract with Ligue 1 club St Etienne.

“I cannot see Malcom leaving us,” Bordeaux manager Jocelyn Gourvennec said recently.

“Malcom will not be leaving this month,” Bordeaux owner Nicolas de Tavernost told today’s L’Équipe.

“There is nothing new,” Bordeaux President Stéphane Martin told Sud-Ouest yesterday.

These quotes all despite the fact that the press continues to whip up a frenzy regarding Malcom’s possible transfer to Arsenal in recent days.

The player’s agents were in London last week before joining up with Malcom in Troyes after Bordeaux’s Ligue 1 win on Saturday.

Malcom then went to Paris, officially to celebrate his birthday, despite the fact that Bordeaux have a very important Ligue 1 match tomorrow against Caen.

Bordeaux representatives remain firm with their position: no club has made a concrete approach for Malcom. President Martin has repeated that the situation is not evolving at that the Brazilian will remain at Bordeaux until the end of the season.

A deal whereby an agreement is reached this month for Malcom to join a top European club followed by him being loaned back to Bordeaux for the rest of the season is something that the Ligue 1 side are interested in. With Sanchez’s departure imminent from Arsenal, one can expect the Premier League outfit to make a bid before the end of the month, but several German sides are also tempted by Malcom’s situation.
Newcastle have failed in a loan bid for Slovakian goalkeeper Martin Dubravka. Sparta Prague don’t want to let him go on a temporary basis.

One of the reasons Arsenal would prefer Alexis Sanchez to go to United instead of City is Henrikh Mkhitaryan.

Sky Sports News has been told United are willing to let Mkhitaryan go to Arsenal as part of the Sanchez deal and Arsene Wenger is a big fan of the attacking midfielder.

Arsenal tried to sign him before he joined Man Utd from Borussia Dortmund for £30m.
